video:video.video:title: Trump says US will move to take enriched uranium if Iran deal is reached
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says Iran wants “to make a deal,” and claimed U.S. envoys have been holding talks with a respected Iranian leader, whom he did not name.

video:video.video:title: ICE officers make their way to airports' TSA checkpoints after Trump order amid partial shutdown
video:video.video:description: The deployments in New Orleans and other cities came as thousands of TSA agents have worked without pay since Congress failed to renew DHS funding last month.

video:video.video:title: Trump places statue of Christopher Columbus near the White House
video:video.video:description: A statue of Christopher Columbus has been placed on the grounds of the Eisenhower Executive Office Building adjacent to the White House.

video:video.video:title: Survivor and relative express anger as trial opens over Greek train crash that killed 57
video:video.video:description: A criminal trial opened in Greece Monday over a train collision that killed 57 people, many of them college students, in a disaster that horrified the country and revealed long-neglected safety failures.

video:video.video:title: Sandra Hüller on singing a Harry Styles song in 'Project Hail Mary'
video:video.video:description: Here's how Sandra Hüller ended up singing Harry Styles' "Sign of the Times" in "Project Hail Mary." The sci-fi film had the second-biggest opening weekend of all-time for a non-franchise movie.

video:video.video:title: Stephen Graham discusses nurturing young talent
video:video.video:description: Stephen Graham says he feels an “obligation” to nurture young stars he works with like Kit Rakusen and Owen Cooper. He stars alongside Rakusen in “The Good Boy.”

video:video.video:title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to reopen crucial Strait of Hormuz; AP explains
video:video.video:description: Trump made the announcement on his Truth Social site Monday, just hours ahead of a deadline later in the day.

video:video.video:title: Lion and tiger cubs make their debut in Bulgarian zoo's baby boom
video:video.video:description: Stara Zagora Zoo is celebrating the birth of three lion cubs. The tiny big cats are the latest addition to a baby boom that includes a new tiger cub, with more young predators expected at the Bulgarian zoo in the coming months.

video:video.video:title: US stock markets jump, oil prices plunge as Trump announces Iran progress
video:video.video:description: Oil prices are falling sharply on Monday, and stock prices are jumping on Wall Street following severe losses elsewhere in the world before Trump’s announcement.

video:video.video:title: Rescue teams in Iran search for survivors in rubble of damaged buildings in Tehran and Urmia
video:video.video:description: The Iranian Red Crescent posted on Monday a video on its social media account showing rescuers searching for survivors under the rubble of destroyed buildings after they were targeted by US-Israeli strikes.

video:video.video:title: Trump says talks with 'a top person' in Iran on ending the conflict went 'perfectly'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the U.S. held “very strong” talks with a "top" Iranian leader and gave Tehran five more days to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes.

video:video.video:title: Emergency teams on site after deadly early morning strike on residential building in Tehran
video:video.video:description: Emergency services worked to remove the rubble of a residential building in northern Tehran, struck by Israeli-US bombardment early Monday.

video:video.video:title: Emmanuel Grégoire, Socialist long involved in local politics, voted new Paris mayor; AP explains
video:video.video:description: Grégoire won over 50% of the votes, beating out prominent conservative contender Rachida Dati who reached 41% while hard-left contender Sophia Chikirou got about 8%.

video:video.video:title: Pilot and copilot killed in collision between jet and fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport
video:video.video:description: Pilot and co-pilot killed and several others badly hurt when an Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway while landing at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.

video:video.video:title: Trump wants ICE agents deployed to airports not to wear masks
video:video.video:description: Federal immigration officers have been seen at the Atlanta airport after President Donald Trump said he’d deploy them during the partial government shutdown.

video:video.video:title: ICE is seen at Atlanta airport amid partial shutdown causing long TSA lines
video:video.video:description: In Atlanta, travelers stuck in lines caused by the partial shutdown react to ICE's presence at the airport.

video:video.video:title: Drivers worldwide struggle with soaring fuel costs
video:video.video:description: Oil prices continued to climb due to the ongoing war in the Middle East. Everyday living could be affected by soaring energy prices.

video:video.video:title: Bodies of Africans were once dug up and sent to Europe for research. Now they are coming home
video:video.video:description: The remains of dozens of Africans whose bodies were dug up and sent to Europe for scientific research long ago were reburied on Monday in South Africa.

video:video.video:title: 'They mean business:' Trump says Iran wants a deal as Iranian officials deny talks
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says Iran "means business" in talks to end the conflict even as Tehran denies any negotiations.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran 'wants to make a deal' but the Islamic Republic denies any talks
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the U.S. was talking with a “respected” Iranian leader and claimed the Islamic Republic was eager for a deal to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Truckers block Bosnia border crossing with Croatia to protest EU Schengen rules
video:video.video:description: The blockage protests EU rules that cap non-EU drivers' stays to 90 days in a 180-day period. Drivers said they were forced to take this action because of what they say are discriminatory rules that limit their stay in the Schengen.

video:video.video:title: ‘Stop, stop, stop’ heard from control tower during LaGuardia Airport collision
video:video.video:description: Audio recordings from a control tower revealed the moment an Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport, killing two pilots.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street rallies after Trump hints at a possible end to war, even as Iran denies talks
video:video.video:description: A cautious relief flowed through financial markets Monday after President Donald Trump claimed the United States has talked with Iran about a possible end to their war.

video:video.video:title: Two people killed in collision between a jet and vehicle at New York's LaGuardia Airport
video:video.video:description: An Air Canada regional jet struck a firetruck on the runway of New York’s LaGuardia Airport late Sunday, killing at least two people, according to authorities.

video:video.video:title: Air Canada president says efforts focused on passengers and crew members after LaGuardia crash
video:video.video:description: An Air Canada jet with more than 70 passengers collided with a fire truck while landing at New York’s LaGuardia Airport, killing two pilots and injuring dozens.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii suffers its worst flooding in decades
video:video.video:description: More than 2,000 people remained without power Sunday afternoon after Hawaii suffered its worst flooding in more than 20 years when heavy rains fell across the islands.

video:video.video:title: BTS: 'Thank you for waiting for us'
video:video.video:description: K-pop supergroup BTS thanks fans for waiting for them after a four-year break. All seven members of the group recently completed South Korea’s mandatory military service, and hope to reclaim their status as one of the world’s biggest pop acts.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds go on strike at major Navy shipbuilder in Maine over wages and benefits
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of designers, clerks and technicians are on strike in Maine at one of the U.S. Navy’s largest shipbuilding contractors. (AP video by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike on bridge leaves large parts of southern Lebanon disconnected from rest of country
video:video.video:description: Monday's strike on the bridge in the southern village of Qaaqaaiyet al-Jisr cut a main link between the southern city of Nabatiyeh and al-Hujair valley region further south.

video:video.video:title: Trump deploys ICE officers to help TSA as airport lines grow
video:video.video:description: ICE officers are being sent to help TSA at some airports as lines grow longer. Here’s what to know.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court sounds skeptical of late-arriving ballots, a Trump target
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court’s conservative majority sounded open to ruling out state laws that allow the counting of late-arriving mail ballots, a target of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 23
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 23rd: two killed in New York airport crash; Trump visits Memphis to tour safety efforts; Starmer tells lawmakers he's aware of talks between US and Iran; CERN attempts to transport antiprotons

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 23
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday March 23rd: Plane collides with vehicle at New York’s LaGuardia Airport; Iran threatens to attack power plants in Middle East; Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish service set on fire in London; Immigration agents to help with airport security.

video:video.video:title: Two confirmed dead in runway collision at New York's LaGuardia Airport
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed and several others badly hurt when an Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway after landing at New York’s LaGuardia Airport late Sunday night, officials said.

video:video.video:title: ICE seen at Atlanta airport after Trump said he'd deploy agents to supplement TSA during shutdown
video:video.video:description: Officers from ICE and Homeland Security Investigations are deployed to the Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.

video:video.video:title: ICE agents seen at Atlanta airport after Trump said he'd deploy agents to assist TSA during shutdown
video:video.video:description: Federal agents are a routine presence at international airports, where Customs and Border Protection officers screen arriving travelers and Homeland Security Investigations agents handle criminal cases tied to smuggling, trafficking and fraud.

video:video.video:title: UK PM, London mayor and chief rabbi condemn attack on Jewish charity ambulances
video:video.video:description: Officers were called to Golders Green, a north London neighborhood with a large Jewish population, after receiving reports of a fire.

video:video.video:title: ICE officers in New Orleans airport after Trump order amid partial government shutdown
video:video.video:description: The deployments in New Orleans and other cities came as thousands of TSA agents have worked without pay since Congress failed to renew DHS funding last month.

video:video.video:title: Trump tours Elvis Presley's Graceland in Memphis
video:video.video:description: “I love Elvis,” President Donald Trump declares during his first visit to the King of Rock and Roll’s Memphis home.

video:video.video:title: 2 pilots killed when jet collides with fire truck at New York's LaGuardia Airport
video:video.video:description: An Air Canada jet carrying more than 70 passengers collided with a fire truck while landing at New York’s LaGuardia Airport, killing two pilots and injuring several others.

video:video.video:title: NTSB on scene at LaGuardia as probe begins into deadly collision
video:video.video:description: The NTSB is at LaGuardia Airport to begin investigating the fatal collision between an Air Canada passenger jet and a firetruck that killed two pilots and injured dozens of others.

video:video.video:title: West Bank man whose home was targeted by settlers angry over alleged delaying of emergency services
video:video.video:description: Palestinian residents in the occupied West Bank accused Israel's army of deliberately preventing the arrival of fire brigades to a fire site.

video:video.video:title: Mike Will Made-It talks getting Miley Cyrus to rap on his hit song '23'
video:video.video:description: Super producer Mike Will Made-It talks working with Miley Cyrus on his 2013 hit “23.” His 15-track, star-studded album out “R3SET,” is out now.

video:video.video:title: Bodies of southern Africans were dug up and sent to Europe for research. Now they are coming home
video:video.video:description: The remains of dozens of Africans whose bodies were dug up and sent to Europe for scientific research long ago were reburied on Monday in South Africa.

video:video.video:title: Air traffic controller stress comes into focus after LaGuardia jet-fire truck collision
video:video.video:description: The collision between an Air Canada jet and an airport fire truck on a runway at LaGuardia Airport has again put the focus on air traffic controllers.

video:video.video:title: Jury finds that Bill Cosby sexually assaulted woman in 1972, awards her more than $19 million
video:video.video:description: A civil jury in California found Monday that Bill Cosby was liable for drugging and sexually assaulting a woman in 1972 and awarded her $19.25 million.

video:video.video:title: Flames engulf historic courthouse in Georgia
video:video.video:description: The local county government posted that everyone has been safely evacuated from the building in Rome, Georgia. It was built in the 1890s.

video:video.video:title: New Mexico trial nears end over children's safety risks on Meta
video:video.video:description: A jury began deliberations Monday in a landmark trial in New Mexico where social media conglomerate Meta is accused of misleading its users about how safe its platforms are for children.

video:video.video:title: 'The Pitt's' Patrick Ball: 'I'm not sure how the writers do it'
video:video.video:description: Patrick Ball stars in the Emmy-award winning HBO Max hospital drama "the Pitt" alongside Noah Wyle. (March 23)

video:video.video:title: Strikes and smoke seen in Beirut as Israeli attacks continue through the night
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military carried out overnight strikes on multiple neighborhoods in Beirut’s southern suburbs on Tuesday, saying it was targeting Hezbollah infrastructure.

video:video.video:title: ‘Stop, stop, stop’ heard from control tower during LaGuardia airport collision
video:video.video:description: In the audio recorded on Sunday night, a voice could be heard telling a vehicle multiple times to 'stop', before a regional jet struck a fire truck on a runway while landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport.

video:video.video:title: UNESCO expresses concern about impact of war on important historical sites in Iran
video:video.video:description: The United Nations’ cultural agency, UNESCO, has expressed concern about the impact of war on important historical sites in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Albanese and von der Leyen hail 'defining moment' in steps toward EU-Australia trade deal
video:video.video:description: The European Union and Australia on Tuesday agreed on the final text of a free trade agreement, two years after negotiations broke down over Australian demands for more red meat market access and complaints about Australian products labeled with traditionally European names such as prosecco.

video:video.video:title: Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin to lead Homeland Security as TSA standoff deepens
video:video.video:description: The Senate confirmed Markwayne Mullin as Homeland Security secretary late Monday, approving President Donald Trump's nominee to take over the embattled department after the firing of Kristi Noem.

video:video.video:title: Dozens killed as Colombian military transport plane crashes with 121 people on board
video:video.video:description: Ambulances continued rushing to Bogota's Central Military hospital late Monday, carrying dozens of injured passengers after a military transport plane crashed in southwestern Colombia.

video:video.video:title: NTSB chair says runway may stay closed for days as investigators sift through 'expansive' debris
video:video.video:description: NTSB chair Jennifer Homendy says runway may stay closed for days as investigators sift through a “tremendous amount of debris.”

video:video.video:title: Sen. Markwayne Mullin confirmed as next DHS secretary
video:video.video:description: Republican Senator Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma has been confirmed as Secretary of Homeland Security. He replaces Kristi Noem.

video:video.video:title: 11th-Century temple left heavily damaged after Cambodia-Thailand border fighting
video:video.video:description: It's been three months since a ceasefire ended bitter border fighting between Cambodia and Thailand, but the damage has left deep scars in an 11th-century temple atop a 525-meter (1,722-foot) cliff in the Dangrek Mountain range.

video:video.video:title: Tel Aviv residents talk about shock of Iranian missile striking neighbouring building
video:video.video:description: Tel Aviv residents recounted their experiences of shock and fear as an Iranian missile struck a nearby building.

video:video.video:title: Why Miley Cyrus has 'a new perspective' on 'Hannah Montana'
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of “Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special,” Miley Cyrus says she now sees her Disney sitcom and character from “a new perspective.”

video:video.video:title: Impact reported in northern Israel after missile warning during the first hours of Tuesday
video:video.video:description: Israeli emergency services and security forces responded early on Tuesday after a hit was reported amid warnings of Iranian missiles being launched.

video:video.video:title: New downpours in Hawaii lead to more flooding on Oahu Island
video:video.video:description: Authorities said hundreds of homes were damaged, along with some schools and a hospital. Gov. Josh Green said the cost of the storm could top $1 billion.

video:video.video:title: Large oil refinery explosion near Texas coast forces residents to shelter in place
video:video.video:description: A large explosion at an oil refinery near the Texas coast on Monday shot plumes of smoke into the air and forced nearby residents to shelter in place, officials said.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 24
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, March 24th: Trump says U.S. and Iran negotiating; LaGuardia plane-fire truck collision under investigation; More long lines at U.S. airports; Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as new Homeland Security Secretary.

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus revisits 'Hannah Montana' from 'a new perspective' for 20th anniversary
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of “Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special,” Miley Cyrus says she now sees her Disney sitcom and character from “a new perspective.” (March 23)

video:video.video:title: 'Impact is huge': Beijing drivers react to fuel price rise as Iran war continues
video:video.video:description: China's gasoline and diesel prices rose on Tuesday due to the conflict in the Middle East, and drivers in the Chinese capital are feeling the squeeze.

video:video.video:title: Polls open as Denmark holds early election following crisis with US over Greenland
video:video.video:description: More than 4.3 million people are eligible to have their say in the vote for the new Folketing, or parliament, in Copenhagen, which is elected for a four-year term.

video:video.video:title: Your old phone is full of precious metals. Don’t throw it away. Do this instead.
video:video.video:description: Been meaning to get rid of your old phone, laptop or other e-waste? From donation and resale to trade-ins and recycling programs, you’ve got options that actually make a difference.

video:video.video:title: AP reports from the scene of an Iranian missile strike in central Tel Aviv
video:video.video:description: An Israeli police superintendent confirmed that the damage to central Tel Aviv was caused by an Iranian warhead with about 100 kilograms (220.5 pounds) of explosives which directly impacted a residential street.

video:video.video:title: New downpours in Hawaii lead to more flooding on Oahu Island
video:video.video:description: The worst flooding to hit Hawaii in two decades has swept homes off their foundations, floated cars out of driveways and left floors, walls and counters covered in thick, reddish volcanic mud.

video:video.video:title: Police investigating antisemitic hate crime after Jewish charity ambulances set on fire in London
video:video.video:description: Four ambulances belonging to Hatzola Northwest, a volunteer organization that provides emergency medical response, were damaged.

video:video.video:title: A pink veil across the fields: Thousands flock to Greece’s peach blossoms
video:video.video:description: Each year the Imathia region of northern Greece celebrates the arrival of spring beneath a spectacular blanket of pink peach tree blossoms.

video:video.video:title: Diplomatic push on Iran war comes after Trump says talks ongoing
video:video.video:description: With thousands more U.S. Marines on their way to the Gulf, both sides firing intense barrages and Iran denying any negotiations are taking place, the war’s tempo remained high a day after Trump delayed his self-imposed deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Iran strikes Israel as Trump says war winds down but no sign of reduced fighting
video:video.video:description: Iranian missiles and drones targeted Israel’s Tel Aviv and sites across the Mideast on Tuesday, even as President Donald Trump said the United States was in talks with the Islamic Republic to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Nuclear research agency CERN runs delicate test on transporting antimatter
video:video.video:description: Scientists in Geneva took some antiprotons out for a spin — a very delicate one — in a truck, in a never-tried-before test drive that has been deemed a success.

video:video.video:title: America 250 coins shown at George Washington's historic home
video:video.video:description: Limited edition U.S. coins featuring designs that celebrate 250 years of American Independence were shown and given out Monday at Mount Vernon, the historic home of President George Washington.

video:video.video:title: Thai court holds gold mine operator liable for toxic runoff in decade-old environmental case
video:video.video:description: A Thai court on Tuesday ruled that the operator of a major gold mine in northern Thailand is responsible for environmental damage and health impacts on nearby villagers.

video:video.video:title: Trump places statue of Christopher Columbus near the White House
video:video.video:description: A statue of Christopher Columbus has been placed on the grounds of the Eisenhower Executive Office Building adjacent to the White House, the latest effort by President Donald Trump’s administration to recognize the controversial explorer.

video:video.video:title: Noah Wyle is getting his next big break - in London
video:video.video:description: At a launch event for HBO Max in London, actor Noah Wyle reveals what it felt like seeing his face on billboards in Piccadilly Circus for his work in medical drama "The Pitt."

video:video.video:title: Aerial video shows scene of collision at New York's LaGuardia Airport
video:video.video:description: Officials say the collision that killed two pilots occurred when the fire truck crossed the tarmac after being given permission to check on another plane.

video:video.video:title: 50 years after Argentina’s bloody coup, families still search for the disappeared
video:video.video:description: Nearly 50 years after Argentina's 1976 coup that led to a repressive dictatorship, families are still searching for the disappeared.

video:video.video:title: HBO stars pick their ideal castmates
video:video.video:description: At the UK launch of HBO Max in London, actors Noah Wyle, Lisa Kudrow and Steve Carell reveal which stars they’d cast on their shows. (March 24)

video:video.video:title: Oklahoma’s governor picks energy executive Alan Armstrong to fill US Senate seat through end of year
video:video.video:description: Gov. Kevin Stitt, who had pledged to pick a “conservative voice” to fill Markwayne Mullin's seat, elevates to the Senate the chairman and former CEO of Williams Companies, a major pipeline operator based in Tulsa.

video:video.video:title: Long lines at Houston airport as Congress considers a partial DHS funding deal
video:video.video:description: Wait times remain high at U.S. airports as senators discuss a plan to end the Homeland Security budget stalemate by funding much of the department, including Transportation Security Administration airport workers.

video:video.video:title: Republicans wrestle with SAVE Act bill debate in Senate
video:video.video:description: Republicans are in the midst of holding the Senate floor and talking about the SAVE Act. (AP Video by Rick Gentilo)

video:video.video:title: Zagreb's festival of lights marks the arrival of spring
video:video.video:description: Croatia’s capital Zagreb is hosting its annual Festival of Lights, an event timed with the spring equinox and designed to mark the transition from winter to longer days and warmer weather.

video:video.video:title: A tale of two Castros: Cubans wonder if their president will be replaced as US pressure grows
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel still has two years left in his term, but some doubt he’ll make it, with concerns he could be replaced by a member of the Castro dynasty.

video:video.video:title: HBO Max actors pick dream guest stars
video:video.video:description: TV stars at the rooftop launch for HBO Max in London choose which of the attending actors they'd like to appear in their show.

video:video.video:title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
video:video.video:description: Asian countries are turning to coal as the Iran war disrupts oil and gas shipments. The continent is exposed because it relies on imported fuel, much of it passing through the Strait of Hormuz — a chokepoint for about a fifth of global oil and natural gas trade.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 24
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, March 24th: a deal could end the Homeland Security budget standoff; Trump lays out conditions to end war in Iran; investigators continue to probe NYC airport collision; humpback whale stuck in shallow German waters.

video:video.video:title: Afghanistan releases national Dennis Coyle held for more than a year
video:video.video:description: Afghanistan's Taliban authorities on Tuesday released American academic Dennis Coyle after holding him for over a year, with the Foreign Ministry saying the release came on the occasion of Eid al-Fitr, the Muslim holiday that marks the end of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan.

video:video.video:title: Early election in Denmark follows crisis over US designs on Greenland; AP explains
video:video.video:description: More than 4.3 million people are eligible to have their say in the vote for the new Folketing, or parliament, in Copenhagen, which is elected for a four-year term.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers try to refloat stranded humpback whale in Germany’s Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: In a race against time, rescue teams at Timmendorfer Strand beach in northern Germany have been trying to refloat a 10-meter-long (30-feet-long) humpback whale since it became stranded in shallow water on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Amazon says Bahrain data center operations disrupted by drone activity
video:video.video:description: Amazon says Bahrain data center operations disrupted by drone activity

video:video.video:title: Iran Red Crescent video shows rescue of man from under rubble, search of strike-hit Tehran building
video:video.video:description: The Iranian Red Crescent on Tuesday posted footage on its social media showing a survivor being rescued from under the rubble of a strike-hit building in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Blind harbor seal named Onion starts a new chapter in Washington state
video:video.video:description: The Point Defiance Zoo & Aquarium in Tacoma, Washington has a new resident -- an 11‑year‑old blind harbor seal named Onion.

video:video.video:title: New DHS Secretary sworn in as deal emerges to end budget standoff and travel disruptions deepen
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says senators are "working on all of that," when asked whether he would back a deal for Department of Homeland Security funding.

video:video.video:title: Life goes on underground in Israel, in scenes both real and surreal
video:video.video:description: When sirens and cellphone alerts blare warnings of missiles incoming from Iran, people in Israel stream into shelters, turning parking garages, metro stations and basements into temporary communities.

video:video.video:title: Longest line at Philly airport? Cheesesteaks, not security
video:video.video:description: Organizers say Philadelphia achieved a new Guinness World Record for the longest line of cheesesteak sandwiches, with 1,291 lined up inside a departure hall to mark National Cheesesteak Day.

video:video.video:title: Jewish charity ambulance service vehicles set on fire in London
video:video.video:description: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity were set on fire early Monday in London in what British police are investigating as an antisemitic hate crime.

video:video.video:title: Federal officers spotted at Houston, Philadelphia and Chicago airports Tuesday
video:video.video:description: As U.S. airports remain jammed with long lines due to short staffing at TSA, federal officers were seen Tuesday at George Bush Intercontinental Airport in Houston, Chicago’s O’Hare International Airport and Philadelphia International Airport.

video:video.video:title: Long lines at Houston airport as Congress considers a partial DHS funding deal
video:video.video:description: Travel disruptions deepened in cities like Houston as senators are racing to salvage a proposal to end the Homeland Security shutdown. (AP Video: Lekan Oyekanmi)

video:video.video:title: What travelers think about long lines and ICE at airports
video:video.video:description: Airport lines are getting longer, and ICE officers are now being deployed at some locations. We asked travelers what they think.

video:video.video:title: Lawyers speak after Supreme Court considers reviving restrictive immigration asylum policy
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court grappled Tuesday with an immigration policy that has been used to turn back migrants seeking asylum at the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Record-smashing heat spreads: 'Basically the entire US is going to be hot'
video:video.video:description: After smashing March heat records in 14 states and the U.S. as a whole, the gigantic heat dome that's baked the Southwest is creeping eastward and may end up being one of the most expansive heat waves in American history, meteorologists and weather historians say.

video:video.video:title: Fire truck in deadly LaGuardia crash lacked equipment needed to trigger warning system
video:video.video:description: Federal investigators say a runway warning system didn’t trigger an alarm before an Air Canada jet and a fire truck collided at New York’s LaGuardia Airport because the fire truck did not have a transponder.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says he didn't know of allegations that ex-lawmaker was lobbying for Venezuela's Maduro
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio testified about his interactions with former Miami congressman David Rivera nearly a decade ago, when his longtime friend was accused of secretly lobbying on behalf of Venezuela’s government.

video:video.video:title: Scale of latest Russian attacks shows Moscow 'has no intention' of ending war, Zelenskyy says
video:video.video:description: A daylight Russian attack hit buildings near St. Andrew’s Church in the western Ukrainian city of Lviv on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Villagers check out remains of large missile that came down in West Bank
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Tuesday inspected the trunk of a massive missile lodged in the rocky ground of a village near the central city of Nablus.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon declares Iran envoy persona non grata as Israeli strikes continue; AP explains
video:video.video:description: The move comes as Lebanon's relations with Iran have deteriorated since the last Israrel-Hezbollah war in 2024, with ties souring further over the past month since the latest war between Israel and the militant group began.

video:video.video:title: Come with me to the Hannah Montana 20th anniversary premiere
video:video.video:description: Miley Cyrus premiered the "Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special" at the El Capitan Theatre in Hollywood, and AP reporter Sarah Jones-Smith is giving you an inside look.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii digs out from another round of flooding after surprise downpour
video:video.video:description: Crews began evaluating damage and clearing debris from a surprise downpour that sent floodwaters raging near downtown Honolulu. (AP video: Audrey McAvoy)

video:video.video:title: New Mexico jury says Meta harms children's mental health and safety, violating state law
video:video.video:description: A New Mexico jury finds that social media conglomerate Meta is harmful to children’s mental health and in violation of state consumer protection law.

video:video.video:title: Indiana TSA agent turns to food bank as paychecks stop during government shutdown
video:video.video:description: Thousands of TSA officers across the country missed their first full paycheck last weekend because of the partial government shutdown.

video:video.video:title: Minnesota officials sue Trump administration over immigration shooting investigations
video:video.video:description: The lawsuit claims the Trump administration has reneged on promises to cooperate with state investigations into the killings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian and Israeli mothers lead dozens in barefoot march for peace in the center of Rome
video:video.video:description: Palestinian mother Reem Al-Hajajreh and Israeli mother Dr. Yael Admi took off their shoes and marched hand-in-hand to call for peace.

video:video.video:title: 'The Pitt's' Patrick Ball: 'I'm not sure how the writers do it'
video:video.video:description: "The Pitt" actor Patrick Ball says the show's writers are often prescient and do a good job of reflecting real-world issues.

video:video.video:title: Floods in Kenya worsen as 2 rivers burst banks, with death toll climbing
video:video.video:description: The Kenyan government said Tuesday that two rivers have burst their banks, flooding farms and displacing families.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV urges dialogue to end Middle East war and violence
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV repeated his call for an end to war in the Middle East on Tuesday, saying dialogue was needed with the violence "getting worse." (AP video: Andrea Rosa)

video:video.video:title: Halle Bailey expresses her love for Italy after shooting another film there
video:video.video:description: Halle Bailey says Italy has "become like my second home now." Her new film "You, Me & Tuscany" hits theaters April 10.

video:video.video:title: First of three ships with aid arrives in Havana as Cuba's crises deepen
video:video.video:description: The first ship of an international flotilla bringing humanitarian aid to Cuba arrived Tuesday in Havana loaded with solar panels, bicycles, food and medicine as the island’s economic and energy crises deepen.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse shows enormous lines for passengers in Houston airport amid partial government shutdown
video:video.video:description: Timelapse shows enormous lines for passengers in Houston airport amid partial government shutdown

video:video.video:title: Indiana TSA agent turns to food bank as paycheck stops during government shutdown
video:video.video:description: Taylor Desert, a TSA agent at the Indianapolis airport, says she may look for another job if the shutdown reaches 60 days.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Jury finds Meta violated New Mexico state law, harming children’s mental health
video:video.video:description: A New Mexico jury has found that social media conglomerate Meta is harmful to children’s mental health and in violation of state consumer protection law.

video:video.video:title: Frederiksen 'disappointed' but hopes to stay on as Danish prime minister after inconclusive election
video:video.video:description: Denmark’s election on Tuesday ended in an inconclusive result that left the prime minister’s future unclear. (AP video: James Brooks, Kostya Manenkov)

video:video.video:title: Asian shares make gains after Trump's pronouncements of negotiations with Iran, analyst says
video:video.video:description: Oil prices fell more than 5% and Asian shares gained on Wednesday over possibilities of a deescalation of the Iran war and negotiations between the United States and Iran.

video:video.video:title: See inside a vast salt mine under Lake Erie helping keep roads clear
video:video.video:description: Below Cleveland, in a subterranean world many surface dwellers don’t know exists, miners extract a crucial winter mineral — salt.

video:video.video:title: Muslims in Indian-controlled Kashmir collect donations to support Iran's war effort
video:video.video:description: More than a thousand miles from Iran, Muslim residents in Indian-controlled Kashmir were collecting donations for Iranians: gold, cash, even utensils and livestock.

video:video.video:title: Iranian military statement mocks US for 'strategic failure', suggests no negotiations over 15-point plan
video:video.video:description: An Iranian military spokesperson mocked U.S. attempts at a ceasefire deal Wednesday, insisting that the Americans were only negotiating with themselves.
